ops.body:
Lane E (E45, ledger b702c6f9) diagnosed and partially fixed the constant decider; pre-registered ship gate NOT met, fix staged but unshipped in workspace cc-steer-we.

Root cause of the collapse: v1 prompt's selection criterion ("most appropriate, helpful, and least disruptive call") hard-codes a silence preference, and opus applies a visible-derailment bar while the gold strata encode preference-level steering. Best candidate c3 (watcher-fire-harm-v2: demanding-lead framing + concreteness test + symmetric costs; panel_labels K=3 majority, PANEL_VOTES=3): run A TPR 0.333 / FPR 0.133 / J 0.20; run B 0.267 / 0.267 / J 0.00; pooled J 0.10, Newcombe CI [-0.119, +0.307]; test-retest kappa 0.734 (vs 0.0 deployed). Non-constant now, and reliability is fixed, but J is not materially > 0 with CI: the 10 never-caught warranted rows are unanimous 3-0 losses under every framing — the render-v2 tail window doesn't carry their steer-worthiness (TPR ceiling ~0.33; recall pushes cost FPR ~0.67). Unblocking options: human blind labels (cc17e0c) to test whether the windows are even human-separable; enrich the packet windows; or accept c3 as a reliability-only improvement with the gate documented informative.
